creator: jhale@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Daniel Engberg from comment #0)
I tried to build libmusicbrainz5 with the new version of libxml2 and your patch and I am getting an error in poudriere:

FAILED: src/CMakeFiles/make-c-interface.dir/xmlParser.cc.o 
/usr/local/libexec/ccache/c++  -I/wrkdirs/usr/ports/audio/libmusicbrainz5/work/.build/src -I/wrkdirs/usr/ports/audio/libmusicbrainz5/work/libmusicbrainz-release-5.1.0-19-ge29cc9a/src -I/wrkdirs/usr/ports/audio/libmusicbrainz5/work/libmusicbrainz-release-5.1.0-19-ge29cc9a/src/.. -I/wrkdirs/usr/ports/audio/libmusicbrainz5/work/libmusicbrainz-release-5.1.0-19-ge29cc9a/src/../include -I/wrkdirs/usr/ports/audio/libmusicbrainz5/work/.build/src/../include -I/usr/local/include/neon -I/usr/local/include/libxml2 -O2 -pipe -DLIBICONV_PLUG -fstack-protector-strong -fno-strict-aliasing  -DLIBICONV_PLUG -O2 -pipe -DLIBICONV_PLUG -fstack-protector-strong -fno-strict-aliasing  -DLIBICONV_PLUG -MD -MT src/CMakeFiles/make-c-interface.dir/xmlParser.cc.o -MF src/CMakeFiles/make-c-interface.dir/xmlParser.cc.o.d -o src/CMakeFiles/make-c-interface.dir/xmlParser.cc.o -c /wrkdirs/usr/ports/audio/libmusicbrainz5/work/libmusicbrainz-release-5.1.0-19-ge29cc9a/src/xmlParser.cc
In file included from /wrkdirs/usr/ports/audio/libmusicbrainz5/work/libmusicbrainz-release-5.1.0-19-ge29cc9a/src/xmlParser.cc:32:
In file included from /usr/local/include/libxml2/libxml/tree.h:1307:
In file included from /usr/local/include/libxml2/libxml/xmlmemory.h:218:
In file included from /usr/local/include/libxml2/libxml/threads.h:35:
In file included from /usr/local/include/libxml2/libxml/globals.h:18:
In file included from /usr/local/include/libxml2/libxml/parser.h:812:
/usr/local/include/libxml2/libxml/encoding.h:31:10: fatal error: 'unicode/ucnv.h' file not found
#include <unicode/ucnv.h>
         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1 error generated.

First, libmusicbrainz5/CMakeLists.txt looks for libxml2 this way:
FIND_PACKAGE(LibXml2 REQUIRED) which will use the old find module for libxml2 included with CMake. It needs to be changed to FIND_PACKAGE(libxml2 REQUIRED) (note all lowercase).

This doesn't fix the problem completely, though. All of the logic in ${LOCALBASE}/lib/cmake/libxml2-2.9.13/libxml2-config.cmake that would potentially expose the ICU includes is only turned on when LIBXML2_SHARED is set to OFF. Even still, if ICU is enabled it only has

list(APPEND LIBXML2_LIBRARIES    ${ICU_LIBRARIES})

I added some logic to supply ICU_INCLUDE_DIRS outside of that block and it seems to work for me. I've attached the patches of what I did to audio/libmusicbrainz5 and textproc/libxml2 to get it to build.
